title: City of Syracuse to Host Public Meeting on Creekwalk Phase 3 Project
summary: The City of Syracuse will host a public meeting on April 27, 2026, to present the preferred trail route and design plans for the Creekwalk Phase 3 extension project, which will extend the trail system south along Onondaga Creek to improve pedestrian and bicycle connectivity, sidewalk safety, and road crossing infrastructure across multiple neighborhoods.

political_signal.description: Public engagement on a capital infrastructure project demonstrating commitment to multimodal transportation, neighborhood connectivity, and pedestrian/cyclist safety aligned with Vision Zero and sustainability priorities.

processing_notes: Author field marked 'Unknown' in source metadata
No budget allocation or dollar amounts disclosed in release
Online feedback portal and extended comment period (through May 29, 2026) suggests structured public input process
Project framed around equity/access (connecting neighborhoods, safer options) and active transportation modes
Brighton Academy venue selection may indicate geographic focus on South Side neighborhoods
